lessons.location: Jiefang Park (解放公园) - Artificial Wetland Area
learningGoals: Identify and name 5 common wetland plants and animals in English.
Understand the definition of an 'ecosystem' through direct observation.
Practice using descriptive adjectives to record nature findings in a field journal.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will go on a nature scavenger hunt to find specific wetland species and create a 'Habitat Map' of the park's pond area. They will use magnifying glasses to look at plant structures and present one 'discovery' to the group.
lessons.safetyNotes: Stay behind the wooden railings near the water; watch for slippery moss on stones.

lessons.location: East Lake (东湖) - Tingtao Scenic Area
learningGoals: Explain how wetlands act as natural water filters using simple sequence connectors (first, then, finally).
Measure water transparency using a basic Secchi disk tool.
Compare 'muddy' vs 'filtered' water samples using comparative adjectives.
lessons.activitySummary: After testing the clarity of East Lake's water, students will work in teams to build a DIY water filtration tower using sand, gravel, and charcoal. They will predict which materials clean the water most effectively.
lessons.safetyNotes: Wear waterproof shoes; do not touch or drink the lake water samples.

learningGoals: Identify 3 local bird species and describe their physical features (beaks, feathers, wings).
Understand the concept of migration and why birds choose Wuhan wetlands as a stopover.
Use binoculars correctly and record data in a bird-watching log.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will use binoculars to observe birds around Moon Lake and the nearby Hanyang cultural area. They will participate in a 'Beak Challenge' game to learn how different beak shapes are adapted for eating different foods.
lessons.safetyNotes: Keep a safe distance from wild birds to avoid disturbing them; stay on designated walking paths.

learningGoals: Identify human-made threats to wetlands like plastic waste and urban noise.
Propose a creative solution to protect the wetland using 'should' and 'must' for suggestions.
Design a 3D model of a 'Sustainable Wetland Park' using recycled and natural materials.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will conduct a mini 'Trash Audit' to see how humans affect Sha Lake. They will then work in groups to sketch and build a model of a wetland that includes features for both people and animals to coexist safely.
lessons.safetyNotes: Use gloves when picking up any trash for the audit; be careful with scissors and glue during the modeling phase.
